answer:
Oh, wow, this actually takes me back to my college days. So, there was this one semester where we had a group project in my business management class. The goal was to develop a marketing plan for a local coffee shop that was struggling to attract new customers. I was teamed up with three other classmates—one was my roommate, and the others I barely knew before the project started.

From day one, we realized we all had pretty different ideas and working styles. At first, things were a little chaotic, no lie. But after some honest conversations—like, just laying everything out on the table—we figured out how to play to each other's strengths. For example, my roommate was super creative, so she handled most of the design stuff. I took care of the research and data analysis since, you know, numbers are kind of my thing.

We met up a couple of times a week—sometimes over pizza—and gradually put together a really solid plan. By the end, we presented our ideas to the coffee shop owner, and he actually decided to try out some of our suggestions. Looking back, I think one big takeaway was learning how to compromise and trust each other. Honestly, it felt amazing to see our teamwork pay off.
title:
Describe a time when you had to work with others to achieve a goal.
What was the goal? 
 Who did you work with? 
 What was the outcome of your teamwork?
